statement: An OCP system can be formalized as a protected/disturbance/correction tuple with exact and asymptotic branches.
next_action: Use as the primary formal language.

statement: If H = S ⊕ D with S orthogonal to D, orthogonal projection onto S exactly recovers the protected component of any state s+d.
next_action: Promote as the clean finite-dimensional backbone.

statement: If S ∩ D is nontrivial, no single-valued recovery map can recover s from x=s+d for all s in S and d in D.
next_action: Promote as the strongest exact no-go statement.

statement: The flow xdot = -k P_D x leaves S fixed and exponentially suppresses D.
next_action: Use as the bridge from exact correction to asymptotic correction.

statement: Knill-Laflamme style exact correction fits OCP when syndrome sectors are orthogonal and recoverable.
evidence_type: standard QEC theorem plus repo-local rewrite
next_action: Keep exact but conditional on KL-type assumptions.

statement: Periodic Helmholtz projection gives an exact OCP recovery operator for divergence-free fields under additive gradient contamination.
next_action: Promote heavily as the best exact continuous example.

statement: GLM divergence cleaning fits OCP as an asymptotic correction architecture rather than an exact projector.
evidence_type: implementation analysis and empirical reduction tests
next_action: Present as a good asymptotic example, not exact correction.

statement: Observer/controller designs instantiate OCP when the protected/disturbance split is invariant and feedback acts only on disturbance coordinates.
There exists one scalar correction-capacity number that nontrivially classifies QEC, MHD projection, GLM, and control architectures.
next_action: Do not promote without a better category-specific definition.

statement: Current optimizer work is a genuine OCP instantiation rather than a loose bridge.
evidence_type: local sibling-repo evidence is too weak

statement: OCP yields practical design rules for building correction operators and recognizing when a correction architecture is underpowered.
next_action: Use as the main practical pitch, but keep the domain assumptions explicit.

statement: OCP gives a meaningful bridge between discrete exact correction and continuous asymptotic correction.
next_action: Promote after upgrading the bridge from framework language to a sharper theorem statement.

statement: If K annihilates S, preserves D, and its restriction to D is exponentially stable, then the flow xdot=-Kx preserves S and asymptotically suppresses D.
next_action: Promote as the strongest next-step theorem beyond the projector case.

statement: If K is self-adjoint positive semidefinite with ker(K)=S and spectral gap lambda_*>0 on S^perp, then ||e^{-tK}x-P_S x|| <= e^{-lambda_* t}||P_D x||.
next_action: Use as the cleanest grounded continuous-time strengthening.

statement: If P_S K P_D is nonzero, then disturbances generically leak into the protected coordinates and the flow is not an OCP correction flow preserving S pointwise.
next_action: Promote as the strongest new linear-flow failure criterion in the repo.

statement: For an exact linear recovery on V = S ⊕ D with correction operator C = I - R, one must have rank(C) >= dim(D) and rank(R) >= dim(S).
next_action: Promote as the cleanest minimum-structure theorem in the repo.

statement: Pairwise orthogonal nonzero exact-correction sectors require at least as many distinguishable correction labels as sectors.
next_action: Keep this branch-specific and avoid overstating it beyond the exact sector model.

statement: OCP supports branch-specific correction-capacity notions even though a universal scalar capacity remains unsupported.
evidence_type: definitions, lower bounds, and examples across branches
next_action: Develop this further before treating it as a mature theory.
